content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
May 17,
1994
Honorable Pete Valdez, Jr.
Councilmember, City of Gilroy
7565 Santa Theresa Drive
Gilroy,
CA
95020
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—94—112
Dear Councilmember Valdez:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice
regarding your responsibilities under the conflict—of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”) •1
Please note that nothing in this letter should be construed
to evaluate any conduct which may have already taken place.
In
addition, this letter is based on the facts presented to us.
The
Ccxmmission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice.
(In re Oglesby
(1975)
1
FPPC Ops.
71.)
QUESTION
May Councilmember Valdez participate in decisions concerning
a proposed city ordinance regulating cardroom operations within
the City of Gilroy?
CONCLUSION
Yes.
Councilmember Valdez may participate in decisions
concerning the proposed city ordinance_regulating cardroom
operations within the City of Gilroy.
FACTS
Councilmember Valdez is a member of the city council of the
City of Gilroy.
The city is considering a revision to the
ordinance which regulates cardrooms in the City of Gilroy.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California.Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000-18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—94--112
Page
2
Mr.
Herman Garcia,
owner of Garcia’s Cardroom,
located in
Gilroy, is a holder of a cardroom permit from the State of
California and the City of Gilroy.
About two and a half years
ago,
Mr. Garcia informed Mr. Valdez about a plan that he and
another individual, Collette Wood, had to make movies.
Mr. Garcia
and Ms.
Wood were seeking financing for their venture.
Mr. Garcia
and Mr. Valdez agreed that in exchange-for services that
Mr. Valdez would perform for the venture,
Mr. Valdez would receive
a 10—percent interest in the partnership.
Mr. Valdez performed
about four hours of work for the partnership, but has not done any
other work for the partnership since then and, specifically, has
not done any work for the partnership in the last twelve months.
Mr. Valdez stated that he would value the partnership at about
$5,000.2
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 provides that no public official at any level
of state or local government shall make, participate in making,
or
in any way attempt to use his or her official position to
influence a governmental decision in which the official knows or
has reason to know he or she has a financial interest.
Section 87103 provides in relevant part:
An official has a financial interest in a
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally,
on the
official or a member of his or her immediate family
or on:
(a)
Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
orThore.
*
*
*
(c)
Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars
($250)
or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
-decision is made.
...
2
This additional information was provided by Mr.
Valdez in a
telephone conversation on April 26,
1994.


File No.
A—94—112
Page
3
The Partnership Interest
Councilmember Valdez’s 10—percent interest in the
partnership, which he valued at $5,000, is worth $500 and,
therefore,
does not reach the threshold level of an investment of
$1,000 in a business entity provided in Section 87103(a).
Accordingly,
Councilmember Valdez’s interest in the partnership
does not disqualify him from participating in decisions concerning
the proposed city ordinance regulating cardroom operations within
the City of Gilroy.
The Sources of Income
Councilmember Valdez received a 10—percent interest in the
partnership in exchange for services that he would perform for the
partnership.
The 10—percent interest represents the payment that
he received for services he had performed and, therefore, is
deemed to be in-kind income to him.
Accordingly,
Mr. Garcia and
Ms.
Wood are sources of income to him.
However,
he performed the
services about two and a half years ago.
Consequently, pursuant
to Section 87103(c),
Mr.
Garcia and Ms.
Wood are not disqualifying
sources of income to him.
Therefore,
Councilmember Valdez’s
economic interests do not disqualify him from participating in
decisions concerning the proposed city ordinance regulating
cardroom operations within the City of Gilroy.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322-5901.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:-
Jeean Ahuja
Counsel,
Legal Division
